ab: Mniq kngkawas mpusal kbkuy mpusal dha cida, seyhu pa mnuda psli prngawan smatang malu wana spatas hangan sediq tnpusu ka nibunso sediq tnpusu.
ch: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列族名的會議。
en: In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

ab: Mnuda ku smiling pprngaw kari bsiyaq balay mbiyax meysa seyhu nibunso u, “Murux posa hangan sediq tnpusu” ka risaw alang Temu Nawi.
ch: 我訪問了長期向政府爭取身分證「單列族名」的部落青年Temu Nawi。
en: I interviewed Temu Nawi, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

ab: Wada ku mhdu prngaw hiya, mniq diyax sngayan ni musa sapah na smiling kari hiya.
ch: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
en: We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

ab: Mniq smiling kari cida, muda pkla knan pusu pnyahan hangan na ka hiya, muda pkla knan tnsapah niya mi alang na uri.
ch: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
en: During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

ab: Slingan mu ka hiya, hmuya ka kasi qulung balay knpriyux hangan Sediq, naqah ka waya smudal posa hangan pa?
ch: 我問他為什麼堅持要改名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
en: I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn’t the old registration method sufficient?

ab: Rmngaw knan ka hiya, alaw dmuwi kari Tlu u uka brahan mdungus bay pkla hangan na, duma sediq u lmawa hiya “Te seyngsey”, wada so ni prana ou balay shlisun uri.
ch: 他對我說，因為用中文字並不能準確的傳達他的名字，其他人還會叫他「鐵先生」，也因此鬧出不少笑話。
en: He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him \"Tie Xian Sheng,\" which resulted in many jokes being made.

ab: Rmngaw ka Temu Nawi: “Spatas hangan sediq tnpusu u kiya ka balay bay hangan nami. Hangan mu pa rmirih yami Sediq Balay, tndadal nami mi hnyuwan nami, so mosa hbaraw bay sediq tnpusu u dhuq smdiyal hangan nami nanaq dungan.”
ch: Temu Nawi說：「原住民語書寫的名字才是我們真正的名字。我的名字代表了我們的族群、我們的家族、我們的身分，希望能有更多族人找回自己的名字。」
en: Temu Nawi said, \"The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group, our family, our identity. I hope more fellow tribespeople can reclaim their own names.\"
